edition.title: Daily Intel Briefing — May 11, 2026
edition.subtitle: Nine signal items across hypersonics and space tracking, strategic nuclear systems, and artificial intelligence.
edition.analyst_note: Today’s indicators point to a defense ecosystem racing to turn concepts into fieldable capacity: counter-hypersonic kill chains, nuclear-enterprise infrastructure, GEO surveillance, and agentic cyber AI are all moving from PowerPoint into procurement, test, and operations.

hypersonics.title: Maverick Targets Hypersonic Kill Chain
hypersonics.summary: The Missile Defense Agency is planning a FY2027 event called Project Maverick to test tracking and engaging a hypersonic glide vehicle flying up the U.S. East Coast. The test is expected to use elevated multi-phenomenology sensor data and a tactical battle manager to prove engage-on-remote mechanics against a maneuvering target.
hypersonics.why_it_matters: This is not just another interceptor test; it is a sensor-fusion and command-and-control test for the hardest part of hypersonic defense. If MDA can close the loop from air- and space-based custody to tactical fire control, Golden Dome’s counter-hypersonic architecture becomes more than a budget line.
hypersonics.source_label: Air & Space Forces: Missile Defense Agency Plans Counter-Hypersonic Test in Fiscal 2027

hypersonics.title: Helios Adds Hypersonic Test Capacity
hypersonics.summary: Kratos selected Odon, Indiana, as the site for Project Helios, a new mid-tier coupled arc jet and laser facility for hypersonic aerothermal materials testing. The facility is designed to bridge laboratory materials work and fielded hypersonic systems by expanding U.S. test bandwidth.
hypersonics.why_it_matters: The United States still suffers from a hypersonic ground-test bottleneck, especially in high-temperature materials and thermal-protection validation. More arc-jet and laser capacity shortens the design-build-test loop and reduces reliance on scarce flight tests for basic survivability questions.
hypersonics.source_label: Kratos: Kratos Names Odon, Indiana as Home of New Hypersonic Test Facility

hypersonics.title: Andromeda Scales GEO Watch
hypersonics.summary: The Space Force raised the ceiling on its Andromeda satellite contract from $1.8 billion to more than $6.2 billion for reconnaissance and surveillance spacecraft. The vehicle will support RG-XX maneuverable GEO-tracking satellites and SG-XX wide-field surveillance satellites as follow-ons or complements to GSSAP and Silent Barker.
hypersonics.why_it_matters: Space domain awareness is becoming a pacing function for missile warning, strategic stability, and counterspace defense. A larger Andromeda ceiling signals that the Space Force expects the GEO threat picture after 2030 to require more persistent custody, more proliferated sensors, and faster replacement of legacy exquisite assets.
hypersonics.source_label: Air & Space Forces: Space Force Adds $4B to Andromeda Contract

nuclear.title: Savannah River Facility Completed
nuclear.summary: NNSA completed the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility, adding infrastructure for nuclear security and weapons modernization. The facility supports safer plutonium processing inside the nuclear security enterprise.
nuclear.why_it_matters: Warhead modernization is increasingly constrained by infrastructure, not just design authority or budget. New plutonium-processing capacity helps NNSA reduce schedule risk across modernization programs while keeping aging facilities from becoming single points of failure.
nuclear.source_label: NNSA: New Facility to Strengthen Nuclear Security and Modernize Weapons

nuclear.title: Sentinel Costs Stay in View
nuclear.summary: A May 6 CRS primer updated Congress on LGM-35A Sentinel, the planned ICBM replacement for Minuteman III. The visible budget summary lists $4.6 billion in FY2027 RDT&E, $2.9 billion in Air Force procurement, and $913 million in NNSA warhead-related funding, while noting IOC has slipped from 2029 to 2030.
nuclear.why_it_matters: Sentinel remains the most politically exposed leg of U.S. nuclear modernization because it combines hardening, command-and-control, missile procurement, and real-estate complexity across hundreds of silos. The updated CRS framing gives Congress a fresh oversight baseline as cost, schedule, and triad-sustainment debates intensify.
nuclear.source_label: CRS: Defense Primer: LGM-35A Sentinel ICBM

nuclear.title: China’s Nuclear Sub Fleet Accelerates
nuclear.summary: USNI Proceedings reports that China produced at least three nuclear-powered submarines in 2025 and may have launched roughly eight Type 093B SSGNs since 2022. The article projects the PLAN submarine force could reach about 70 boats by 2027 and 80 by 2035, roughly half nuclear powered.
nuclear.why_it_matters: China’s shift from mostly conventional submarines to a larger nuclear-powered undersea force changes the geography of deterrence and naval crisis management. More SSNs, SSGNs, and SSBNs give Beijing longer reach beyond the first island chain and complicate U.S. assumptions about persistent undersea advantage.
nuclear.source_label: USNI Proceedings: Going Nuclear, Getting Bigger, and Going Beyond

ai.title: CAISI Expands Frontier Testing
ai.summary: The Commerce Department’s CAISI signed or updated frontier-model testing agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, xAI, Anthropic, and OpenAI. The agreements cover pre-deployment evaluations, post-deployment assessments, and targeted research into national-security implications.
ai.why_it_matters: The U.S. government is moving from voluntary dialogue toward structured measurement of frontier capabilities before release. That gives policymakers earlier warning on cyber, biosecurity, autonomy, and misuse thresholds while preserving access to the labs most likely to set the technical frontier.
ai.source_label: Axios: U.S. Ramps Up Frontier AI Testing

ai.title: Scale Wins $500M Pentagon Deal
ai.summary: Scale AI won a $500 million Pentagon contract through the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office, a fivefold expansion over its September 2025 agreement. The award supports AI integration into military decision-making and data-processing workflows.
ai.why_it_matters: The data layer is now being treated as a strategic procurement category, not a back-office accessory to model buys. If CDAO can scale reliable data pipelines and decision-support tooling, the Pentagon’s AI adoption curve moves closer to operational units and away from isolated pilots.
ai.source_label: The Next Web: Scale AI Wins $500M Pentagon Contract

ai.title: Army Pushes Agentic Cyber AI
ai.summary: The Army convened 14 senior cybersecurity executives at the Pentagon for AI Tabletop Exercise 2.0, focused on agentic AI for cyber defense in a future Indo-Pacific crisis. Participants included AWS, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, U.S. Cyber Command, Army Cyber Command, and the Army Cyber Institute.
ai.why_it_matters: The Army is treating machine-speed cyber defense as a near-term warfighting requirement rather than a research topic. Its stated 30–90 day rapid-prototyping path suggests the service wants existing commercial AI capabilities pushed into operational cyber units quickly, with policy gaps handled in parallel.
ai.source_label: U.S. Army: AI Tabletop Exercise Focused on Cyber Defense

archive.summary: Maverick Targets Hypersonic Kill Chain, Helios Adds Hypersonic Test Capacity, Andromeda Scales GEO Watch, Savannah River Facility Completed, Sentinel Costs Stay in View, China’s Nuclear Sub Fleet Accelerates, CAISI Expands Frontier Testing, Scale Wins $500M Pentagon Deal, and Army Pushes Agentic Cyber AI.

archive.summary: Project Maverick Sets a Hypersonic Kill-Chain Test, DRDO Pushes Scramjet Endurance Past 1,200 Seconds, Anduril Buys Its Own Hypersonic Flight Test Lane, Nuclear Modernization Hits the Budgetary Python, Washington Pitches Post-New START Risk Controls, Pantex Gets a Modern High-Explosives Hub, CAISI Gets Pre-Release Access to Frontier Models, Pentagon Opens Classified Networks to Seven AI Firms, and DIU Wants AI Aiming Help Against Drones.

archive.title: Daily Intel Briefing — May 9, 2026
archive.summary: Project Maverick, Space Based Interceptor work, India missile testing ambiguity, NNSA rapid-design pressure, triad modernization strain, Pantex infrastructure, CAISI frontier-AI testing, DIU counter-drone targeting, and IMF warnings on AI-enabled cyber risk.
